criteria_results.criterion: Migration 021 adds effort_score INT CHECK (1-5) nullable and impact_score INT CHECK (1-5) nullable to steps table
criteria_results.evidence: supabase/migrations/021_prioritization_scores.sql line 5-7: ALTER TABLE steps ADD COLUMN effort_score INT CHECK (effort_score BETWEEN 1 AND 5), ADD COLUMN impact_score INT CHECK (impact_score BETWEEN 1 AND 5). Matches migration 007 pattern exactly (verified 007 side-by-side). Nullable by default (no NOT NULL constraint).

criteria_results.criterion: Migration 021 adds effort_score INT CHECK (1-5) nullable and impact_score INT CHECK (1-5) nullable to touchpoints table
criteria_results.evidence: supabase/migrations/021_prioritization_scores.sql line 9-11: ALTER TABLE touchpoints ADD COLUMN effort_score INT CHECK (effort_score BETWEEN 1 AND 5), ADD COLUMN impact_score INT CHECK (impact_score BETWEEN 1 AND 5). Same nullable pattern.

criteria_results.criterion: Step interface in src/types/database.ts includes effort_score: number | null and impact_score: number | null
criteria_results.evidence: database.ts lines 93-94: effort_score: number | null and impact_score: number | null added after target_maturity: number | null (line 92) per special_instructions. Type matches nullable INT column exactly.

criteria_results.criterion: Touchpoint interface in src/types/database.ts includes effort_score: number | null and impact_score: number | null
criteria_results.evidence: database.ts lines 197-198: effort_score: number | null and impact_score: number | null added after position_y: number (line 196) and before created_at: string (line 199), exactly matching special_instructions placement.

criteria_results.criterion: Steps PATCH route includes effort_score and impact_score in EDITABLE_FIELDS
criteria_results.evidence: src/app/api/v1/steps/[id]/route.ts lines 21-22: 'effort_score' and 'impact_score' present in EDITABLE_FIELDS array (follows existing maturity_score and target_maturity pattern at lines 19-20).

criteria_results.criterion: Touchpoints PATCH route includes effort_score and impact_score in EDITABLE_FIELDS
criteria_results.evidence: src/app/api/v1/touchpoints/[id]/route.ts lines 16-17: 'effort_score' and 'impact_score' present in EDITABLE_FIELDS array after 'position_y'.

criteria_results.criterion: npx tsc --noEmit passes cleanly after changes
criteria_results.evidence: BUILD_RESULT_1.json verification.typecheck: { status: 'pass', errors: 0 }. BUILD_RESULT_2.json: same. BUILD_RESULT_3.json: same. All three slots report zero TypeScript errors.

criteria_results.criterion: DialogDescription in the Duplicate Workspace dialog includes the workspace name dynamically
criteria_results.evidence: settings/page.tsx line 273: DialogDescription contains 'A full copy of "{workspace.name}" will be created including all tabs, sections, steps, connections, teams, roles, people, and tools.' workspace.name is sourced from useWorkspace() at line 36.

criteria_results.criterion: Settings page body text paragraph also includes the workspace name for consistency
criteria_results.evidence: settings/page.tsx line 245: paragraph reads 'Create a full copy of "{workspace.name}", including all tabs, sections, steps, connections, teams, roles, people, and tools.' Uses JSX " entities to produce typographic quotes around the name.

criteria_results.criterion: Each runbook card shows a text label like '3 / 5' next to or below the progress bar
criteria_results.evidence: runbooks-list-view.tsx line 124: {resolved} / {total} rendered inside the flex container alongside the progress bar div (line 119).

criteria_results.criterion: Text uses existing text hierarchy (text-[var(--text-secondary)] or text-[var(--text-tertiary)])
criteria_results.evidence: runbooks-list-view.tsx line 124: className includes text-[var(--text-tertiary)]. Consistent with surrounding card metadata text at line 111 which also uses text-[var(--text-tertiary)].

criteria_results.criterion: Text shows resolved count (completed + skipped) out of total, matching playbook-view.tsx pattern
criteria_results.evidence: runbooks-list-view.tsx line 94: const resolved = completed + skipped. Line 124 renders {resolved} / {total}. The 'resolved' variable correctly sums completed (line 91) + skipped (line 92). tabular-nums applied for stable digit width.

checks.check: Auth route — middleware.ts unchanged, login redirect intact
Workspace context — workspace-context.tsx not in changed files, no regression risk
Canvas nodes — flow-canvas.tsx, step-node.tsx unchanged, no regression risk
Settings page — only string content in clone dialog and body paragraph changed, no structural or state logic altered
Runbooks list — only flex wrapper added to progress bar section and span appended; existing filter, routing, empty state, badge logic untouched
Step PATCH API — EDITABLE_FIELDS extended (additive); existing field handling unchanged
Touchpoint PATCH API — EDITABLE_FIELDS extended (additive); existing field handling unchanged
TypeScript integrity across all changed files — all three BUILD_RESULTs report typecheck pass with 0 errors
Lint — no new warnings or errors in any slot (1 pre-existing addEdge warning only)

browser_checks_skipped: Clone dialog render with workspace name

improvements_found.observation: Step count now appears twice on each runbook card: once in the metadata row as '{resolved}/{total} steps' (IMP-014, line 114) and again next to the progress bar as '{resolved} / {total}' (IMP-046, line 124). Both are correct but slightly redundant. The formats also differ slightly: no spaces around slash + 'steps' suffix vs spaces around slash + no suffix.
improvements_found.design_principle: Nielsen H8: Aesthetic and minimalist design — only show what's needed
improvements_found.suggested_approach: Remove the metadata row count ('{resolved}/{total} steps') and keep only the progress bar label, which provides the same information in context. Alternatively, make the formats consistent (both 'N / M' or both 'N/M steps').

improvements_found.observation: The Delete Workspace action at settings/page.tsx line 157 still uses the native browser confirm() dialog, while the Duplicate Workspace action uses a polished Radix Dialog (IMP-028). This is a pre-existing inconsistency — the delete action deserves the same level of dialog UX (with clear consequence description and named buttons).
improvements_found.design_principle: Nielsen H4: Consistency and standards — same interaction pattern for destructive actions
improvements_found.suggested_approach: Add a confirmDeleteOpen state + Radix Dialog for delete, mirroring the clone dialog pattern. Include workspace name in the description: 'Permanently delete [workspace name]? This cannot be undone.'

notes: Playwright unavailable — Chrome launched but immediately exited ('Opening in existing browser session' error). Performed enhanced static analysis covering all 12 acceptance criteria, regression baseline checks, and suite checks via source code reads. All criteria pass statically. Browser-side regression checks (canvas render, panel interactions, console errors, export) could not be verified and are flagged as skipped. Recommend running Playwright in a standalone terminal session to complete browser-side regression.
